file.content:
{
  "project": "Marketify",
  "branchName": "ralph/marketify-mvp",
  "description": "Mobile marketplace (Expo + RN + Supabase) where creators claim bounties from listers. v1 MVP per docs/product-plan.md \u00a73.1, design language per docs/design.md, schema per docs/tech-architecture.md \u00a74.7. No payments, no OAuth \u2014 Apify scrapes power creator metrics.",
  "userStories": [
    {
      "id": "US-001",
      "title": "Bootstrap Expo + TypeScript app skeleton",
      "description": "Initialize the Expo app at the repo root so future stories have something to build into.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Run `npx create-expo-app@latest . --template blank-typescript --no-install` (skip install \u2014 install with bun after)",
        "Install deps with `bun install` (bun is available; do NOT use npm/yarn)",
        "Add `app.json` config: name=Marketify, slug=marketify, scheme=marketify, ios.bundleIdentifier=com.marketify.app, android.package=com.marketify.app",
        "Add `tsconfig.json` strict mode + paths: `@/*` \u2192 `src/*`",
        "Create `src/` with empty `components/`, `screens/`, `lib/`, `types/` subfolders (each with a `.gitkeep`)",
        "Add scripts to package.json: `typecheck` (tsc --noEmit), `lint` (expo lint)",
        "`bun run typecheck` passes",
        "Commit"
      ],
      "priority": 1,
      "passes": true,
      "notes": ""
    },
    {
      "id": "US-002",
      "title": "Install core runtime deps (Supabase, Reanimated, Navigation, NativeWind)",
      "description": "Pre-install the libraries the rest of the build depends on so subsequent stories don't keep adding deps.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Install: @supabase/supabase-js, @react-native-async-storage/async-storage, react-native-url-polyfill, expo-router, react-native-reanimated, react-native-gesture-handler, react-native-svg, lucide-react-native, expo-haptics, expo-linking, expo-constants, expo-font, expo-status-bar, react-native-mmkv",
        "Configure Reanimated babel plugin in `babel.config.js`",
        "Configure expo-router entry point in package.json (`main: expo-router/entry`)",
        "`bun run typecheck` passes",
        "App boots in iOS simulator without red-screen (verify with mobile-mcp: launch + take screenshot)",
        "Commit"
      ],
      "priority": 2,
      "passes": true,
      "notes": ""
    },
    {
      "id": "US-003",
      "title": "Create Supabase project + apply baseline extensions/enums migration",
      "description": "Stand up the Supabase project and the foundational schema enums per docs/tech-architecture.md \u00a74.7.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Use Supabase MCP `list_organizations` then `create_project` (name=marketify-mvp, region=us-east-1) if no project exists; else use existing",
        "Save project ref + URL + publishable key into `.env` keys SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (do NOT print values back to chat)",
        "Apply migration `us_003_baseline_enums`: enable extensions (pgcrypto, pg_cron if available), create enums per tech-architecture.md \u00a74.7 (user_role, listing_status, application_status, submission_status, scrape_mode, etc.)",
        "Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` \u2014 note any findings (expected: low/none for enums)",
        "Run `generate_typescript_types` \u2192 write to `src/types/supabase.ts`",
        "Commit migration + types"
      ],
      "priority": 3,
      "passes": true,
      "notes": ""
    },
    {
      "id": "US-004",
      "title": "Migration: users + creator_profiles + lister_profiles + social_links",
      "description": "Identity tables per tech-architecture.md \u00a74.7. No Supabase Auth \u2014 custom JWT.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Migration `us_004_identity_tables` creates: users, creator_profiles, lister_profiles, social_links exactly per docs/tech-architecture.md \u00a74.7",
        "Include all denormalized creator metric columns (tiktok_follower_count, instagram_avg_views_last_10, instagram_follower_count, etc.)",
        "Foreign keys + unique constraints per spec",
        "Regenerate TypeScript types",
        "`get_advisors({type:'security'})` \u2014 fix any errors before commit (RLS will be added in a later story; expect a 'rls disabled' warning, that is acceptable here)",
        "Commit"
      ],
      "priority": 4,
      "passes": true,
      "notes": ""
    },
    {
      "id": "US-005",
      "title": "Migration: listings + listing_versions + pre/post conditions",
      "description": "Campaign-side tables per tech-architecture.md \u00a74.7 including the version table the cascade trigger relies on.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Migration `us_005_listing_tables` creates: listings, listing_versions, listing_pre_conditions, listing_post_conditions",
        "Include cache columns `min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_instagram` as plain integer (NOT generated) on listings, with btree indexes",
        "Foreign keys, NOT NULL, defaults exactly per spec",
        "Regenerate TypeScript types",
        "Commit"
      ],
      "priority": 5,
      "passes": true,
      "notes": ""
    },
    {
      "id": "US-006",
      "title": "Migration: applications + submissions tables",
      "description": "Pipeline tables that drive the creator/lister workflow.",
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "Migration `us_006_pipeline_tables` creates: applications, submissions per spec",
        "Include status enums, foreign keys, the reserved revision-request column on submissions per tech-architecture.md",
        "Regenerate TypeScript types",
        "Commit"
      ],
      "priority": 6,
      "passes": true,
      "notes": ""
    },

content.content:
630	        "Commit"
631	      ],
632	      "priority": 42,
633	      "passes": true,
634	      "notes": "2026-04-17: src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx (new) wraps BottomSheet at snapPoints=[0.85] with header 'You're applying to' + listing title, optional pitch TextInput (hard-clamped to 280 chars via maxLength), live counter, disclaimer, and 'Send application' CTA. invokes the apply-to-listing edge function (US-041) with body { listing_id, cover_note? }. Decodes structured errors via FunctionsHttpError context Response (Codebase Pattern #98) and maps INELIGIBLE/LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED/ALREADY_APPLIED/LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE/LISTING_NOT_FOUND to per-failure 'error' toasts; INELIGIBLE summarises the first failed_condition. submittingRef + setSubmitting both gate fast double-taps (Pattern #106). On success the parent fires onApplied which closes the sheet, shows the success toast, then defers router.replace('/(creator)/applications') by 350ms so the BottomSheet's spring-out animation settles before the route swap. Failure path closes sheet + reloads listing so the eligibility rail and CTA reflect the new server reality. Keyboard handling: KeyboardAvoidingView is unreliable inside RN Modal (the modal opens its own window so KAV's measured frame and keyboard frame disagree); replaced with direct Keyboard.addListener(keyboardWill/DidShow|Hide) that drives a paddingBottom on the inner View so the CTA stays above the iOS keyboard. AC/spec divergences: (1) AC says 'pitch' but the canonical column + edge-function field is cover_note (docs/tech-architecture.md \u00a75.5); (2) AC routes to '/(creator)/applied' but the actual layout tab is '/(creator)/applications' (US-031); (3) AC error code is 422 but the edge function returns 403/409/404 with structured codes \u2014 mapped per spec. Verified end-to-end on iOS sim: empty sheet, populated pitch, submit \u2192 row in applications table (status=pending, cover_note matches), navigation to Applied tab. Code-reviewer's 5 findings all addressed before commit (drop FOCUS_KEYBOARD_FALLBACK \u2192 event-only listener; useRef submit lock; 350ms setTimeout before router.replace; already_applied variant 'info' \u2192 'error' for taxonomy consistency; maxLength=COVER_NOTE_MAX_LEN exact + remove dead overLimit/inputWrapError). Screenshots: us-042-sheet-with-keyboard.png (sheet at 0.85, pitch typed, CTA above software keyboard), us-042-submitted.png (Applied tab post-submit). Spec gap: docs/design.md \u00a74.3 mock shows 0/160 counter while story AC says 280 \u2014 used 280 per story AC."
635	    },
636	    {
637	      "id": "US-043",
638	      "title": "My Applications tab (creator)",
639	      "description": "Lists creator's applications grouped by status.",

643	        "Each row: campaign title, lister handle, status pill, applied_at relative",
644	        "Tap row \u2192 navigates to listing detail",
645	        "Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot each segment with seeded data",
646	        "Commit"
647	      ],
648	      "priority": 43,
649	      "passes": false,
650	      "notes": ""
651	    },
652	    {
653	      "id": "US-044",
654	      "title": "Realtime subscription for application status changes (creator)",
655	      "description": "Push status flips into the UI via Supabase Realtime.",
656	      "acceptanceCriteria": [
657	        "Subscribe to applications WHERE creator_user_id = me on the Applied tab",
658	        "On UPDATE: optimistically move the row to the new segment; fire toast 'Your application was approved/rejected'",
659	        "Mobile-mcp verification: from Supabase Studio (or via execute_sql) flip a seeded application to approved while the screen is open \u2014 confirm UI updates without manual refresh",
660	        "Commit"
661	      ],
662	      "priority": 44,
663	      "passes": false,
664	      "notes": ""

input.description: Find US-036 and US-038 blocks in prd.json

content.content:
410:      "notes": "CampaignCard props intentionally minimal (title, listerHandle, priceCents, currency, preConditionSummary, status, onPress) \u2014 the \u00a76 inventory lists platforms[]/eligibility/variant/counts but the story AC enumerates only the 5 rendered fields. Deferred the richer prop set (same precedent as US-025 Button variant + US-026 Chip variant) \u2014 will land when the first caller actually needs them (US-038 Discover feed for eligibility/platform badges, US-054 My Campaigns for lister-side counts). SkeletonCard stores layout width in a `useSharedValue` rather than React state so the worklet avoids stale-capture flicker on first layout (code-reviewer catch \u2014 promoted to Codebase Patterns). Mid-press shadow-collapse screenshot deliberately NOT captured (same timing limitation as US-025 Button) \u2014 rest-state + tsc/lint/code-review are authoritative verification."
529:      "notes": "Implemented. Migrations: us_035_manage_social_link_fn (RPC), us_035_metric_snapshots_self_read_stale (RLS widen: fresh+stale), us_035_social_links_handle_check (CHECK regex mirrors edge-fn). Edge function manage-social-link deployed (verify_jwt=false, handler enforces Marketify JWT). Spec gap: AC names 'metrics_stale_at' but no such column exists; derived staleness from metric_snapshots.status='stale' (flipped by us_011 hourly mark-metrics-stale cron). Apify scrape-on-add deferred to US-036 per AC scope. Code-reviewer: issue 1 (duck-typing on FunctionsHttpError) kept per Codebase Pattern 98 to stay canonical with US-033/US-034; issue 2 (no DB handle validation) addressed via CHECK constraint migration. Mobile-mcp: empty state + Add Handle bottom sheet verified (us-035-empty.png, us-035-add-sheet.png); populated state verification deferred (stale JWT from prior session; covered by US-020 Apify scrape + US-034 signup tests)."
532:      "id": "US-036",
544:      "notes": "2026-04-17: code implemented + reviewed + quality-gated (tsc/lint). Drag verification blocked by pre-existing PGRST301 RLS rejection on PostgREST \u2014 the project JWT secret has drifted from MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET so every authed query under the custom HS256 token fails with JWSInvalidClaims. Re-confirmed blocker on 2026-04-17 via direct PostgREST probe with a real creator JWT (401 / PGRST301). Affects any logged-in screen, not just US-036. Re-run verification after the secret is re-aligned (dashboard Project Settings \u2192 JWT Settings) \u2014 the pull-to-refresh code itself is unchanged. 2026-04-17 (3rd iter): re-probed via scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts (signup-lister \u2192 PostgREST /rest/v1/lister_profiles with returned token) \u2192 401 PGRST301 \"No suitable key or wrong key type\". Drift still in effect. Supabase MCP exposes no tool to rotate the project JWT secret; requires dashboard operation (Project Settings \u2192 API \u2192 JWT Settings) \u2014 cannot be fixed from within Ralph. Probe script kept at scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts so future iterations can re-check in one command (`bun run scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts`)."
558:      "notes": "2026-04-17: profile screen renders username (@dev_lister) + email from AuthContext and org_name from lister_profiles with '\u2014' fallback. Sign out invokes AuthProvider.signOut which clears SecureStore and routes back to (auth) role picker via app/index.tsx role guard \u2014 verified via mobile-mcp. org_name row shows '\u2014' in verification because US-036's JWT drift blocker still rejects PostgREST reads; the fallback + error toast are the designed behavior under that failure mode."
561:      "id": "US-038",
574:      "notes": "2026-04-17: skipped this iteration \u2014 same JWT-drift blocker as US-036. Re-probed today by signing a real creator JWT via auth-signup-lister edge fn \u2192 PostgREST /rest/v1/lister_profiles returned 401 PGRST301 'No suitable key or wrong key type'. The Discover feed AC hits PostgREST directly for listings + creator_profiles under the signed-in creator's JWT; until the project JWT secret is re-aligned with MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET in the Supabase dashboard, every authed row read fails at the gateway and the feed would show an error state regardless of code. Moving forward with US-040 which routes through the get-listing-detail edge function and is NOT impacted."
604:      "notes": "2026-04-17: Campaign detail at app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx posts to get-listing-detail (US-039), renders hero card + sample videos (link-out via Linking.openURL) + Eligibility rail with check/X icons + 'What to film' post-condition bullets + Brief section + sticky footer CTA gated per docs/design.md \u00a74.2 (eligible: orange 'Apply to collab'; ineligible: disabled 'Not eligible yet' + bullet summary of failed conditions; has_active_application: Applied pill + disabled CTA; non-active listing: CTA hidden, inactive banner at top). Apply action is a stub toast per AC scope \u2014 US-041/US-042 land the real apply. Seed migration us_040_seed_ineligible_listing adds Mega-Luxe listing with 3 PRE thresholds that fail seed_creator. Feed has a __DEV__ launcher (Codebase Pattern #96) that routes to both seeded listings so the screen is reachable before US-038. Three screenshots: us-040-eligible-acme.png (green checks, 'Apply to collab' CTA), us-040-ineligible-acme.png (2 fails against Acme thresholds), us-040-ineligible-luxe.png (3 fails against the new Mega-Luxe seed). Spec gap: docs/design.md \u00a74.2 references a lister handle in the header \u2014 get-listing-detail does not yet expose a lister_profiles join, deferred. Code-reviewer's two Important findings were dismissed after verification: (1) operator CHECK on listing_conditions already permits 'contains' (migration applied cleanly, confirmed via pg_constraint); (2) sticky footer safe-area inset is handled by the TabBar in (creator)/_layout.tsx which already absorbs the home indicator \u2014 screenshots on iPhone 17 Pro sim confirm clean spacing, and no existing screen uses useSafeAreaInsets so adding one would break convention."

content.content:
115	- **Run cheap checks before upstream oEmbed/scraper calls in edge functions; return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` (not 500) when the upstream itself fails.** Order inside an endpoint that relies on a third-party URL validator: JWT → role → parse body → regex-classify URL → DB lookups → status/permission gates → affirmation/eligibility check → upstream oEmbed → RPC. This is both a cost optimization (don't burn TikTok rate-limit slots on requests that will 4xx regardless) and a DDoS-amplifier guard (an unauth POST must not be able to force an upstream fetch). Split the error codes: `500 SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` / `500 DB_ERROR` for our-fault failures, `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` for upstream unavailability — clients that auto-retry benefit because upstream outages are usually retryable, DB bugs rarely are. Document BOTH codes in the contract comment. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` oEmbed section + error envelope header.
116	- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
117	
118	---

## 2026-04-17 07:38 — US-001
input.new_string:
- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
- **PostgREST embeds silently NULL joined columns when the target table's RLS filters the caller out — always audit every `.select('..., related!fk(col)')` embed against the related table's RLS policies.** Failure mode: the embed syntax succeeds, returns rows, and populates the parent columns; the embedded field just comes back as `null` or `[]` under RLS because PostgREST applies the policies per-row transparently. Highest-risk embeds on this project: (a) `users!lister_id(username)` where `users_self_select` is self-only — every cross-tenant handle read (creator viewing lister handle, lister viewing applicant handle) will null out; (b) `listing_versions!...(...)` where `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active' OR caller=lister` — any historical/closed listing version the caller doesn't own will null; (c) `listings!...(title)` where `listings_public_read` requires `status='active'` — same closed-listing nullout. Rule: before writing a PostgREST embed, check the target table's RLS; if the policy wouldn't return the specific row under the caller's role, replace the embed with a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that does its own ownership check. DO NOT widen the self-only policy to "return usernames if handle is public" — that leaks the full row shape; DEFINER + narrow column projection is the correct primitive. Ref: Codebase Pattern #40 (`submission_reuse_count`), Codebase Pattern #110 (`apply_to_listing_rpc`), and `public.list_my_applications()` from US-043.
- **SECURITY DEFINER "list-my-rows-with-foreign-context" RPC skeleton.** Contract: `returns table(<flat columns including the cross-tenant bits>)`, `language sql stable security definer set search_path = ''`, body is a straight `select ... from public.<owned_table> t left join public.<foreign_table> f on ... where t.<owner_col> = public.current_user_id() order by ...`. Grants: `revoke all ... from public`, `revoke all ... from anon`, `grant execute ... to authenticated`. Service-role is intentionally not granted — it already has BYPASSRLS and wouldn't use this shape. Why `stable`, not `volatile`: the function reads but doesn't write, so PostgREST can plan around it and query planner caches can reuse results within a statement. Why `sql` over `plpgsql`: the body is one statement, no control flow, no GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS — inlines better. Verified pattern via a transactional `begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.<fn>();` block that confirms (a) the DEFINER bypass returns the cross-tenant columns, (b) the ownership check still gates other users' rows (swap the sub claim to a different creator and assert zero rows). Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`. Applies to any future "my X with lister/creator/reviewer context" list screen.
- **Screen-RPC naming convention: `public.list_my_<things>` for caller-scoped list reads, `public.<action>_rpc` for mutations, `public.<thing>_<metric>(arg)` for aggregates.** Precedents: `public.list_my_applications()` (US-043) / `public.apply_to_listing_rpc(...)` (US-041) / `public.submission_reuse_count(uuid)` (US-009). Keep the `list_my_*` functions argumentless (the caller is implicit via `current_user_id()`) — passing a caller UUID would re-introduce the spoofing surface the helpers exist to eliminate. Keep them in the `public` schema (not `app_private`) because they need to be callable via PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`; `app_private` is reserved for server-side triggers + cron bodies. The `authenticated` grant is mandatory — without it PostgREST returns 42501 "permission denied for function" and the MCP `execute_sql` test path (runs as `postgres`) silently passes, masking the bug.
- **Segmented control a11y: `accessibilityRole="tablist"` wrapper + `accessibilityRole="tab"` on each button + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}`**, NOT the Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `checked`. Chip is for multi-select filter rows where each pill is independent; a segmented control is single-select and semantically is a tab bar. iOS VoiceOver reads "tab, Pending, selected, 1 of 4" for the tablist shape — correct under Apple's a11y guidance. `selected` is the right state (not `checked`) because the user isn't toggling a boolean, they're picking one of N mutually-exclusive views. Inline the control rather than promoting to a shared primitive until a second screen needs it — premature abstraction hurts more than a ~40-line duplicate. Ref: `SegmentControl` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (US-043).
- **Enum-to-UI-bucket maps live as a static `Record<DBEnum, UISegment>` next to the screen that owns the mapping, not in a shared constants file.** `application_status` has 6 values; the Applied tab shows 4 segments (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled), with `withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed` all bucketing to "cancelled" per design.md §3.1 label + the AC's 4-segment design. Type the map as `Record<Database['public']['Enums']['application_status'], Segment>` so adding a 7th enum value trips tsc at the map literal. Do NOT collapse the three cancellation variants in the DB (they carry distinct semantic meaning for future admin/analytics use); collapse only at the UI layer. Same pattern applies to any status enum whose DB shape is richer than the screen's tab count. Ref: `STATUS_BUCKET` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx`.

---

## 2026-04-18 00:38 — US-043
- New screen `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (~290 lines) replaces the placeholder: `<SafeAreaView>` → `<ScrollView>` → "Applied" display header → inline `SegmentControl` (4 pills: Pending / Approved / Rejected / Cancelled, accessibilityRole='tab' + accessibilityState={selected}) → content area. Content states: loading (two SkeletonCards), error ("Couldn't load applications."), empty-per-segment (bespoke copy), or a list of `ApplicationRowCard`s. Each card: campaign title (listing.title → version snapshot title → "Untitled campaign"), `@lister_handle`, StatusPill, "Applied {formatRelativeTime}" — tap routes to `/(creator)/listing/{listing_id}`. 4-segment UI mapped from the 6-value `application_status` enum via a typed `STATUS_BUCKET: Record<ApplicationStatus, Segment>` (withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed → 'cancelled').
- New migration `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`: SECURITY DEFINER SQL function `public.list_my_applications()` returns table of flat joined rows (id, status, created_at, listing_id, listing_title, lister_handle, version_title). Filters by `a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()`. Grants: revoke all from public/anon, grant execute to authenticated. Required because a PostgREST embed `.select('..., lister:users!lister_id(username), version:listing_versions!...(snapshot)')` would silently NULL `lister_handle` for every row (users RLS is self-only) and the version snapshot for non-active listings (listing_versions RLS gates on listing.status='active' OR caller=lister).
- New seed migration `supabase/migrations/20260418010000_us_043_seed_applications.sql`: 4 deterministic-UUID applications for the seed creator — pending/Acme, approved/Luxe, rejected/Acme, cancelled_listing_edit/Luxe — sized so the partial unique `applications_open_uniq(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status IN ('pending','approved')` does not collide (pending+rejected can coexist on Acme; approved+cancelled_listing_edit can coexist on Luxe because the partial index excludes `cancelled_listing_edit`). Idempotent via `on conflict (id) do nothing`.
- Verified via SQL under a simulated creator JWT (`begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.list_my_applications();`) — returns all 4 rows with populated listing_title + lister_handle + version_title, confirming the DEFINER bypass works and the ownership check still gates.
- Files changed:
  - `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (was placeholder; now ~290 lines)
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418010000_us_043_seed_applications.sql` (new)
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql` (new)

toolUseResult.oldString:
- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.

## 2026-04-17 07:38 — US-001
toolUseResult.newString:
- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
- **PostgREST embeds silently NULL joined columns when the target table's RLS filters the caller out — always audit every `.select('..., related!fk(col)')` embed against the related table's RLS policies.** Failure mode: the embed syntax succeeds, returns rows, and populates the parent columns; the embedded field just comes back as `null` or `[]` under RLS because PostgREST applies the policies per-row transparently. Highest-risk embeds on this project: (a) `users!lister_id(username)` where `users_self_select` is self-only — every cross-tenant handle read (creator viewing lister handle, lister viewing applicant handle) will null out; (b) `listing_versions!...(...)` where `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active' OR caller=lister` — any historical/closed listing version the caller doesn't own will null; (c) `listings!...(title)` where `listings_public_read` requires `status='active'` — same closed-listing nullout. Rule: before writing a PostgREST embed, check the target table's RLS; if the policy wouldn't return the specific row under the caller's role, replace the embed with a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that does its own ownership check. DO NOT widen the self-only policy to "return usernames if handle is public" — that leaks the full row shape; DEFINER + narrow column projection is the correct primitive. Ref: Codebase Pattern #40 (`submission_reuse_count`), Codebase Pattern #110 (`apply_to_listing_rpc`), and `public.list_my_applications()` from US-043.
- **SECURITY DEFINER "list-my-rows-with-foreign-context" RPC skeleton.** Contract: `returns table(<flat columns including the cross-tenant bits>)`, `language sql stable security definer set search_path = ''`, body is a straight `select ... from public.<owned_table> t left join public.<foreign_table> f on ... where t.<owner_col> = public.current_user_id() order by ...`. Grants: `revoke all ... from public`, `revoke all ... from anon`, `grant execute ... to authenticated`. Service-role is intentionally not granted — it already has BYPASSRLS and wouldn't use this shape. Why `stable`, not `volatile`: the function reads but doesn't write, so PostgREST can plan around it and query planner caches can reuse results within a statement. Why `sql` over `plpgsql`: the body is one statement, no control flow, no GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS — inlines better. Verified pattern via a transactional `begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.<fn>();` block that confirms (a) the DEFINER bypass returns the cross-tenant columns, (b) the ownership check still gates other users' rows (swap the sub claim to a different creator and assert zero rows). Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`. Applies to any future "my X with lister/creator/reviewer context" list screen.
- **Screen-RPC naming convention: `public.list_my_<things>` for caller-scoped list reads, `public.<action>_rpc` for mutations, `public.<thing>_<metric>(arg)` for aggregates.** Precedents: `public.list_my_applications()` (US-043) / `public.apply_to_listing_rpc(...)` (US-041) / `public.submission_reuse_count(uuid)` (US-009). Keep the `list_my_*` functions argumentless (the caller is implicit via `current_user_id()`) — passing a caller UUID would re-introduce the spoofing surface the helpers exist to eliminate. Keep them in the `public` schema (not `app_private`) because they need to be callable via PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`; `app_private` is reserved for server-side triggers + cron bodies. The `authenticated` grant is mandatory — without it PostgREST returns 42501 "permission denied for function" and the MCP `execute_sql` test path (runs as `postgres`) silently passes, masking the bug.
- **Segmented control a11y: `accessibilityRole="tablist"` wrapper + `accessibilityRole="tab"` on each button + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}`**, NOT the Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `checked`. Chip is for multi-select filter rows where each pill is independent; a segmented control is single-select and semantically is a tab bar. iOS VoiceOver reads "tab, Pending, selected, 1 of 4" for the tablist shape — correct under Apple's a11y guidance. `selected` is the right state (not `checked`) because the user isn't toggling a boolean, they're picking one of N mutually-exclusive views. Inline the control rather than promoting to a shared primitive until a second screen needs it — premature abstraction hurts more than a ~40-line duplicate. Ref: `SegmentControl` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (US-043).
- **Enum-to-UI-bucket maps live as a static `Record<DBEnum, UISegment>` next to the screen that owns the mapping, not in a shared constants file.** `application_status` has 6 values; the Applied tab shows 4 segments (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled), with `withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed` all bucketing to "cancelled" per design.md §3.1 label + the AC's 4-segment design. Type the map as `Record<Database['public']['Enums']['application_status'], Segment>` so adding a 7th enum value trips tsc at the map literal. Do NOT collapse the three cancellation variants in the DB (they carry distinct semantic meaning for future admin/analytics use); collapse only at the UI layer. Same pattern applies to any status enum whose DB shape is richer than the screen's tab count. Ref: `STATUS_BUCKET` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx`.

lines: - **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.

- **Run cheap checks before upstream oEmbed/scraper calls in edge functions; return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` (not 500) when the upstream itself fails.** Order inside an endpoint that relies on a third-party URL validator: JWT → role → parse body → regex-classify URL → DB lookups → status/permission gates → affirmation/eligibility check → upstream oEmbed → RPC. +- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no new findings after RPC migration — only pre-existing INFO on `public.events`) / mobile-mcp ⚠ (UI renders correctly; populated-segment screenshots blocked by the same JWT drift that has blocked US-036 + US-038 — the RPC call fails at the PostgREST gateway with PGRST301 before reaching the function body). Code remains review-ready for when the dashboard fix lands.

+- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) (confidence 92) original PostgREST embed `lister:users!lister_id(username)` would silently null `lister_handle` for every row under the self-only `users_self_select` policy; fixed by switching to the SECURITY DEFINER RPC that joins under its own ownership check. (2) (confidence 85) `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active'` so the snapshot title would null for cancelled-listing-edit / rejected-against-closed rows; same RPC fix covers this. Both findings rated IMPORTANT; no findings rated CRITICAL or dismissed.

+- Spec gap: docs/product-plan.md + docs/design.md:151 call the tab `/(creator)/applications`; the US-043 AC text says `app/(creator)/applied.tsx`. Docs win — matches the canonical URL already wired into US-031's TabBar. The tab label "Applied" is preserved.

+- JWT drift blocker still in effect: `bun run scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts` returns 401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type". Every screen that reads from PostgREST under the custom Marketify JWT remains unverifiable on-device until Project Settings → API → JWT Settings is re-aligned with `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (human-performed dashboard action, not executable from Ralph).

+  - **Audit PostgREST embeds against the target table's RLS BEFORE writing the .select().** The silent-nullout failure mode is high-damage low-visibility: no error, no warning, just a `null` field. The checklist for this project specifically: `users!<fk>` cross-tenant reads are always silent-nulled by `users_self_select`; `listing_versions!<fk>` reads are silent-nulled for non-active listings; `listings!<fk>(title)` reads are silent-nulled for non-active listings. If your screen needs ANY of those cross-tenant fields, you need a DEFINER RPC, not an embed. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.

+  - **The "list my rows with foreign context" RPC is a reusable skeleton.** argumentless, SQL-language, STABLE, DEFINER, `set search_path=''`, left-joined, filtered by `current_user_id()`, granted to authenticated only. Copy this skeleton for US-056 (creator inbox), US-054 (lister campaigns list), US-057 (lister applicants list) — all three have the same cross-tenant-read-under-self-only-RLS shape. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.

+  - **Segmented controls are tablists, not checkbox groups.** When a control is single-select + mutually-exclusive, `accessibilityRole="tablist"` + per-button `"tab"` + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}` is the correct a11y shape. The Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `state={{ checked }}` is only correct for multi-select filters. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.

+  - **DB enums can outgrow UI segments — a static typed Record bridges them.** `application_status` has 6 values; the tab shows 4 segments. A `Record<ApplicationStatus, Segment>` literal with all 6 keys explicitly enumerated means tsc catches the day a 7th enum value lands; collapsing the DB shape to match the UI would lose the distinction between user-initiated withdrawal and lister-initiated cancellation. Keep the DB rich, collapse at the UI. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.

+  - **Skipping `passes:true` because of an upstream env blocker is the right call.** US-036 + US-038 + now US-043 are all mature implementations waiting on a dashboard-side JWT secret alignment. Pretending they're "done" would corrupt the progress signal for the human reviewer; pretending they're "not started" would lose the implementation work. The `passes:false` + detailed notes pattern (with re-run probe script + rationale) is how we keep the ledger honest. Same posture will apply to any future story whose verification path goes through PostgREST under the creator/lister JWT until the drift is resolved.

- Bootstrapped Expo blank-typescript template at repo root; configured app.json (name/slug/scheme + ios.bundleIdentifier / android.package = com.marketify.app); tsconfig strict + `@/*` → `src/*`; added `typecheck` + `lint` scripts.
- Created `src/{components,screens,lib,types}/` each with `.gitkeep`; added `src/index.ts` stub (`export {};`) to satisfy `expo lint`.
